---
title: "journal"
author: "Hayden Simm"
date: "2026-03-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

We are deciding to work in the tidyverse, so we loading in the data using "read_csv" 

possible things to investigate:
- look at how different tissues are activated with the different acclimation conditions
- look at acclimation and body condition 
- MANOVA 

Boxplot: plotted the assay temp by enzyme activity and body condition. Grouped the values by 
acclimation temperature. 

Unsure why when I group values by Acclimation_salinity the boxplots shift when its the same output values even with the different acclimation conditions. 

Few outliers in the body condition plot. 10 degrees acclimated have better body condition than the 24 degree acclimated. 

Do we include salinity acclimation as a predictor? The only test condition we have is the change in temperature..

the diagnostic plots for the Lm for body condition (response), temperature and salinity acclimation and assay temperature(predictors)  all are valid
